title: These are the beaches closed in Mass. as the heat wave hits
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Massachusetts has closed beaches due to bacteria levels following sewer main breaks in Haverhill releasing untreated wastewater.
Beaches affected include Plum Island in Newburyport, Sandy Point, and multiple others updated hourly by the state.
Closures are monitored by the Department of Public Health; factors include rainfall and harmful algae blooms.

title: 17 books to read for America’s 250th anniversary
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Local booksellers and Boston.com staff curated 17 books for America's 250th anniversary.
The list includes new releases and modern classics about America's founding and development.
Booksellers from three independent shops in Greater Boston contributed recommendations.

title: Brockton to implement curfew in response to shootings after World Cup matches
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Brockton will impose a curfew from 10 p.m. Friday to 5 a.m. Saturday due to shootings after World Cup matches.
No entry to bars after 7 p.m. and alcohol sales will cease at 9:30 p.m. Friday night in Brockton.
Brockton requests National Guard support due to violence following Cape Verde's World Cup games and related celebrations.

title: Bruins bring back Connor Clifton on 2-year contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins sign defenseman Connor Clifton to a two-year contract with an average annual cap hit of $2.25 million.
Clifton, 31, previously spent five seasons with the Bruins and played for the Sabres and Penguins in recent years.
Clifton's return could mean the end of Andrew Peeke's time with the Bruins as he is currently a free agent.

title: 4 things to know about Mike Conley Jr. after reported one-year deal agreement with the Celtics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Celtics reportedly agreed to a one-year deal with 38-year-old veteran guard Mike Conley Jr.
Conley is the son of former Olympic gold medalist Mike Conley Sr., who also represented him early in his career.
Conley has won the NBA Sportsmanship Award four times, a record since the award's inception in 1996.

title: Alec Baldwin – at Tanglewood this weekend – talks ’30 Rock,’ Boston accents, and New England movies
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Alec Baldwin will narrate Aaron Copland's 'Lincoln Portrait' with the Boston Symphony Orchestra at Tanglewood on July 5.
Baldwin, a part-time Vermont resident with ties to New England, expresses a passion for classical music and narration.
Baldwin discussed his experiences with Boston accents, filming 'Outside Providence,' and his role on NBC's '30 Rock.'

title: Bruins trade Joonas Korpisalo to Rangers in a cap-clearing move
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins traded goalie Joonas Korpisalo to the Rangers, clearing $3 million in cap space for the next two years.
In return, Boston received prospect Kalle Vaisanen and a 2028 fourth-round draft pick from the New York Rangers.
Michael DiPietro, AHL MVP, is expected to become backup goalie, reducing costs with a $812,500 cap hit.

title: State commission disqualifies GOP candidates for AG, Lt. Gov. after signature fraud allegations
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: The State Ballot Law Commission disqualified GOP candidates Anne Manning Martin and Michael Walsh over signature fraud allegations.
Martin's signatures were challenged by Mass Democratic Party and opponent Shawn Oliver, invalidating 1,279 of her 10,692 signatures.
Walsh's signatures were questioned by forensic expert Jennifer Naso; 1,021 of his 10,677 signatures were deemed invalid.

title: Cambridge scraps its most controversial proposed alcohol rule changes
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Cambridge License Commission scraps proposed earlier last call and 30-minute wait between drink orders after public backlash.
Proposed rules deemed too restrictive, leading to planned feedback sessions with business owners and license holders.
Commission will discuss possible alcohol rule updates in August and September meetings, aiming for alignment with state laws.

title: 3 generations of R.I. family died in Pawtucket accident
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Three Pawtucket family members died when their car drove into the Seekonk River during a driving lesson.
The victims were a 45-year-old woman, a 22-year-old woman, and a 2-year-old girl.
Investigators found the vehicle's brakes were functioning and deemed the incident a tragic accident.
